exampleSentencesTranslated: He has a headache, therefore he is not going to work today.
She studied very hard, therefore she got a high score.
It is raining heavily, therefore I bring an umbrella when going out.

content.sentence: Trời mưa lớn, ___ chúng tôi không đi chơi.

translation.en: It rains heavily, therefore we do not go out.

notes:
do đó means therefore / as a result. It shows a result that comes logically from what was just said.

It’s a linking phrase, usually placed between two clauses, often after a comma. It sounds slightly formal and is common in writing or careful speech.

Very close in meaning to vì vậy and cho nên, but do đó feels a bit more formal and less conversational.

example.es: Hoy está lloviendo, por lo tanto no voy a salir.
example.en: It is raining today, therefore I am not going outside.
example.vi: Hôm nay trời mưa, do đó tôi không đi ra ngoài.

exampleSentences: Anh bị đau đầu, do đó anh không đi làm hôm nay.
Chị đã học rất chăm chỉ, do đó chị đã đạt điểm cao.
Trời mưa rất lớn, do đó em mang theo ô khi ra ngoài.
notes_md:
**do đó** means *therefore / as a result*. It shows a result that comes logically from what was just said.

It’s a linking phrase, usually placed between two clauses, often after a comma. It sounds slightly formal and is common in writing or careful speech.


Very close in meaning to **vì vậy** and **cho nên**, but **do đó** feels a bit more formal and less conversational.

content.sentence: Tôi đã học bài, ___ tôi hiểu rõ.

translation.en: I studied the lesson, therefore I understand clearly.
